$comment: GENERATED, READ-ONLY org-wide gate-suite audit index (the Truthful Attribution protocol §4). Authoritative enforcement lives in each repo's committed .github/gate-suite.json (read by the truthful-attribution-gate at the merged SHA). This index is regenerated by `node scripts/gate-suite-index.mjs` from config/gate-suite-inventory.json during the monthly audit; nothing reads it at merge time. Do NOT hand-edit — run the generator. A CI self-check regenerates and byte-compares (`--check`) to catch drift. `repos: []` means no inventoried repo has committed a suite yet (the inventory scanned is named in `inventory`), NOT that there is nothing to audit.

auditObligation.sampleRule: 10% of eligible gate-arm merges since the last audit, min 5, capped at all eligible
auditObligation.evidence: closing comment on a recurring `Gate-suite audit YYYY-MM` tracking issue
auditObligation.bumpRule: the same commit that updates lastAuditedAt must update auditEvidence (gate-checked at the version-bump rule)
auditObligation.staleness: the truthful-attribution-gate WARNS gate-arm merges when lastAuditedAt > 35 days and FAILS them when > 65 days; human-arm (tier=maintainer Reviewed-by) merges stay possible
